events.name: sas
events.syllabus:
========================================
PROJECTILE MOTION - JEE NOTES
========================================

1. INTRODUCTION
---------------
Projectile motion is the motion of an object projected into air under the influence of gravity alone.

Assumptions:
- Only gravity acts (no air resistance)
- Acceleration is constant (g ≈ 9.8 m/s² downward)

----------------------------------------

2. TYPES OF PROJECTILES
------------------------

1. Oblique Projectile:
   - Thrown at an angle θ with horizontal

2. Horizontal Projectile:
   - Thrown horizontally (θ = 0°)

----------------------------------------

3. RESOLUTION OF VELOCITY
-------------------------

Initial velocity u at angle θ:

Horizontal component:
u_x = u cosθ

Vertical component:
u_y = u sinθ

----------------------------------------

4. MOTION ALONG X-AXIS (HORIZONTAL)
-----------------------------------

No acceleration (a_x = 0)

Velocity:
v_x = u cosθ (constant)

Displacement:
x = (u cosθ) t

----------------------------------------

5. MOTION ALONG Y-AXIS (VERTICAL)
---------------------------------

Acceleration = -g

Velocity:
v_y = u sinθ - gt

Displacement:
y = (u sinθ)t - (1/2)gt²

----------------------------------------

6. TRAJECTORY EQUATION
----------------------

Eliminate time t:

y = x tanθ - (g x²) / (2u² cos²θ)

This represents a PARABOLA.

----------------------------------------

7. TIME OF FLIGHT (T)
---------------------

T = (2u sinθ) / g

----------------------------------------

8. MAXIMUM HEIGHT (H)
---------------------

H = (u² sin²θ) / (2g)

----------------------------------------

9. HORIZONTAL RANGE (R)
-----------------------

R = (u² sin2θ) / g

Important:
- Maximum range when θ = 45°
- Complementary angles (θ and 90°-θ) give same range

----------------------------------------

10. VELOCITY AT ANY POINT
-------------------------

v = √(v_x² + v_y²)

Direction:
tanφ = v_y / v_x

----------------------------------------

11. EQUATION AT HIGHEST POINT
-----------------------------

At top:
v_y = 0

Time to reach top:
t = (u sinθ) / g

----------------------------------------

12. HORIZONTAL PROJECTILE
--------------------------

Initial velocity only in x-direction:

Time of fall:
t = √(2h/g)

Horizontal distance:
x = u t

----------------------------------------

13. IMPORTANT RESULTS
---------------------

1. Range is maximum at 45°
2. Time of flight depends only on vertical component
3. Horizontal motion is uniform
4. Vertical motion is uniformly accelerated

----------------------------------------

14. FREQUENT JEE CONCEPTS
-------------------------

- Finding range on inclined planes
- Relative velocity in projectiles
- Maximum height comparisons
- Motion from moving platforms
- Projectile on different levels

----------------------------------------

15. COMMON MISTAKES
-------------------

- Using wrong angle (sin vs cos confusion)
- Ignoring vertical acceleration
- Mixing up formulas for range and height
- Not resolving velocity properly

----------------------------------------

16. TIPS FOR JEE
---------------

- Always split motion into x and y components
- Use symmetry of motion
- Remember key formulas (T, H, R)
- Draw diagrams for clarity

----------------------------------------

END OF NOTES
========================================

plan.strategy: Given the time constraint, we will focus on high-yield topics and prioritize the most critical concepts to maximize the score. We will also make sure to take regular breaks to maintain productivity and motivation.

today_tasks.task: Solve Oblique Projectile Motion Problems (Quick Win)
today_tasks.reason: Solving oblique projectile motion problems will help you understand how to apply the concepts to different scenarios.
Take a break to recharge and refocus.
Understanding the trajectory equation is essential for solving problems involving projectile motion.
Practicing horizontal projectile motion problems will help you reinforce your understanding of the concepts.

roadmap.focus: Projectile Motion Basics, Oblique Projectile Motion, and Trajectory Equation
roadmap.goal: Understand the basics of projectile motion, solve oblique projectile motion problems, and analyze the trajectory equation.

roadmap.focus: Horizontal Projectile Motion, Time of Flight, and Maximum Height
roadmap.goal: Understand horizontal projectile motion, calculate time of flight, and determine maximum height.

adaptive_rules: If tasks are skipped, adjust the priority of remaining tasks to ensure high-yield topics are covered.
If ahead of schedule, increase the depth of study for each topic to ensure comprehensive understanding.
